---
title: "Viewership Report: Game of Thrones"
author: "Linxiao Mu"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Introduction

*Game of Thrones* is an American fantasy drama television series based on George R. R. Martin's novel series *A Song of Ice and Fire*. The show aired on HBO from 2011 to 2019 and consisted of eight seasons.

![Promotional image or logo of *Game of Thrones*](images/got_image.png)

## Data source

The data used in this report describes the average number of U.S. viewers per season of *Game of Thrones*. The values are measured in millions of viewers and are based on publicly available viewership tables from Wikipedia.

## Basic statistics

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("data/got_viewership.csv")
df["season_to_season_change"] = df["avg_viewers_millions"].diff()

df
```

```{python}
summary = df["avg_viewers_millions"].describe()
summary
```

## Viewership over time

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
plt.plot(df["season"], df["avg_viewers_millions"], marker="o")
plt.xlabel("Season")
plt.ylabel("Average viewers (millions)")
plt.title("Average U.S. viewership of Game of Thrones by season")
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
```

## Season-to-season changes

```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
plt.bar(df["season"], df["season_to_season_change"])
plt.xlabel("Season")
plt.ylabel("Change in average viewers (millions)")
plt.title("Season-to-season change in viewership")
plt.axhline(0)
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
```

## Observed changes

The average viewership of *Game of Thrones* increased strongly over time. In season 1, the show had an average of 2.52 million U.S. viewers. By season 8, this number had increased to 11.99 million viewers.

```{python}
start = df.loc[df["season"] == 1, "avg_viewers_millions"].iloc[0]
end = df.loc[df["season"] == 8, "avg_viewers_millions"].iloc[0]
increase = end - start
percent_increase = increase / start * 100

print(f"The average viewership increased by {increase:.2f} million viewers between season 1 and season 8.")
print(f"This represents an increase of approximately {percent_increase:.1f}%.")
```

The largest season-to-season increase happened between season 6 and season 7. The average viewership increased from 7.69 million to 10.26 million viewers, which is a rise of 2.57 million viewers.

This suggests that the show became increasingly popular as it approached its final seasons. Unlike many long-running television series, *Game of Thrones* did not show a gradual decline in audience size. Instead, its average viewership reached its highest point in the final season.

## AI use statement

Minor help from Generative AI was used to plan the structure of this report, prepare a draft of the explanatory text, and suggest Python code for the charts. The data, calculations, charts, and final wording were reviewed and edited manually.
